title: Kenyan Runner Sabastian Sawe Breaks Two-Hour Barrier in London Marathon
summary: Sabastian Sawe, a 30-year-old Kenyan athlete, made history at the London Marathon by becoming the first person to run a marathon in under two hours in a competitive race. His time of 1:59:30 shattered the previous world record of 2:00:35 set by Kelvin Kiptum in 2023. Sawe was already on world record pace at the halfway mark (1:00:29) and accelerated in the second half. Debutant Yomif Kejelcha also finished under two hours in 1:59:41, becoming the second man to achieve this feat in race conditions. Half marathon world record holder Jacob Kiplimo finished third in 2:00:28. In the women's race, Ethiopia's Tigst Assefa improved her own world record for a women-only field with a time of 2:15:41. The men's wheelchair race saw Swiss athlete Marcel Hug win his eighth London Marathon title, tying David Weir's record. Catherine Debrunner also retained her women's wheelchair title. Sawe expressed joy and excitement after the race, having told BBC earlier that breaking the record was 'only a matter of time.'

keywords.explanation: A person who is competing for the first time in a particular event.
Broken or destroyed completely, often used metaphorically to mean beating a record by a large margin.
A race where all participants are trying to win and the conditions are the same for everyone.
Running at a speed that would result in a new world record if maintained.

background_read: The London Marathon is an annual marathon event held in London, England, since 1981. It is one of the six World Marathon Majors. The men's world record has been broken several times in recent years. Eliud Kipchoge ran under two hours in 2019 but under controlled conditions, not in a competitive race. Kelvin Kiptum set the previous record of 2:00:35 in 2023. Sabastian Sawe had won the London Marathon in 2024 with a time of 2:02:27 before breaking the record in 2025. The event also includes wheelchair races and elite women's races.
Article_Structure: The article reports on Sabastian Sawe's historic sub-two-hour marathon at the London Marathon. Main points include Sawe's winning time of 1:59:30, breaking Kelvin Kiptum's record, and Yomif Kejelcha also finishing under two hours. The purpose is to inform readers of this milestone in athletics. Evidence includes official times and quotes from Sawe. Author credibility is high as BBC is a reputable news source. Methodology involves reporting from the event with athlete interviews.

perspectives.description: Sawe expressed happiness and pride in his achievement, having trained hard and believed it was only a matter of time.
This achievement marks a new era in marathon running, as the two-hour barrier has been broken in a competitive race for the first time.
